context_values.response.implement:
Implemented Slice 1 for `tui:shell` in [events.rs](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/crates/myosu-tui/src/events.rs). The public `EventLoop::new()` path stays intact, but the module now has an internal injectable event-stream seam plus headless tests for tick, key, resize, async update, and cloned update senders, replacing the ignored TTY-only proof. I also added the curated implement-stage artifacts [implementation.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/outputs/tui/shell/implementation.md), [verification.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/outputs/tui/shell/verification.md), and [integration.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/outputs/tui/shell/integration.md).

Verification passed with:
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/myosu-cargo-target cargo test -p myosu-tui events:: -- --include-ignored`
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/myosu-cargo-target cargo test -p myosu-tui shell::`

context_values.last_response: The current `tui:shell` slice is still Slice 1, but its first proof gate is now actually runnable. I added a checked-in shell implementation proof script at [tui-shell-implement.sh](/home/r/.fabro/run
context_values.response.fixup:
The current `tui:shell` slice is still Slice 1, but its first proof gate is now actually runnable. I added a checked-in shell implementation proof script at [tui-shell-implement.sh](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/fabro/checks/tui-shell-implement.sh), pointed the bootstrap wrapper [tui-shell.sh](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/fabro/checks/tui-shell.sh) at the same exact command set, and added the matching Fabro implementation assets at [tui-shell.fabro](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/fabro/workflows/implement/tui-shell.fabro), [tui-shell.toml](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/fabro/run-configs/implement/tui-shell.toml), and [myosu-tui-shell-implementation.yaml](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/fabro/programs/myosu-tui-shell-implementation.yaml). The key fix was forcing `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/myosu-cargo-target-tui-shell`, because the ambient exported target dir in this environment is read-only.

I also refreshed the curated shell artifacts at [implementation.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/outputs/tui/shell/implementation.md), [verification.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/outputs/tui/shell/verification.md), and [integration.md](/home/r/.fabro/runs/20260320-01KM755GFB9SS6HFPKZYFWVJDP/worktree/outputs/tui/shell/integration.md) so they honestly record the stale verify failure, the new checked-in proof path, and the preserved shell contracts. I left `quality.md` and `promotion.md` untouched as requested.

Verification: `./fabro/checks/tui-shell-implement.sh` passed, and `./fabro/checks/tui-shell.sh` passed. That reran the exact selector sequence for `events::`, `shell::`, `all_game_types_have_schema`, `shell_draw_`, and `is_plain_text`, all exit 0. The next approved slice remains Slice 2: the Lobby-to-Game shell integration test.
